Title: The G-protein β subunit SlGB1 regulates tyramine-derived phenolamide metabolism for shoot apex growth and development in tomato

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native locus disrupted by CRISPR/Cas9 for knockout; TRV-based VIGS for silencing; CaMV 35S promoter for complementation/overexpression of SlGB1 and Arabidopsis AGB1 in tomato
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Condine Red (main); some assays in Moneymaker and NahG background
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or restore SlGB1 activity to preserve shoot apex viability, plant architecture, fruit set, and seed yield. Avoid loss-of-function editing of SlGB1. For engineering, deploy functional SlGB1/AGB1 alleles and combine with moderated suppression of downstream phenolamide pathway regulators only if needed; monitor shoot apex integrity and reproductive traits.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SlGB1 suppresses excessive tyramine-derived phenolamide accumulation and is essential for normal shoot apex growth, survival, and fruit set; loss of function causes massive N-P-CT/N-FT accumulation, shoot apex death, stunting, and yield reduction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout, biallelic mutants, VIGS silencing, complementation with SlGB1 and Arabidopsis AGB1, multiomics (metabolomics/RNA-seq/proteomics), targeted LC-MS metabolite quantification, protein interaction assays, subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling developmental stage was critical; shoot apices sampled mainly at 10 DPS when lethality initiated. Plants grown under 500 µmol m−2 s−1 light, 14/10 h photoperiod, 24/21 °C day/night, 80% RH. NahG background used to test SA contribution.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves, roots, stems, and shoot apices; high in shoot apices
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In tomato, expressed broadly with high expression in shoot apices
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bHLH79; AGB1 (functional ortholog used for complementation context)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics (LC-MS; 1,032 metabolites; no public accession reported); RNA-seq (Illumina; no accession reported); shotgun proteomics (no accession reported)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato and Arabidopsis Gβ proteins are functionally conserved at complementation level, but species differ in presence of a THT-mediated tyramine-derived phenolamide pathway, explaining distinct gb1 phenotypes. SA also increased in mutants, but NahG tests indicate SA is not the sole cause of apex death.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of SlGB1 causes seedling lethality/severe apex death, stunted growth, smaller fruits, fewer fruits, fewer/smaller seeds, and reduced biomass/yield. Excess downstream pathway activation is deleterious.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Heterotrimeric G-protein beta subunit; WD40-repeat protein with 7 WD40 domains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGB1 is required for tomato shoot apex survival and normal reproductive development. 2. SlGB1 loss elevates tyramine-derived phenolamides, especially N-P-CT and N-FT, through derepression of THT5/THT8-related metabolism. 3. SlGB1 interacts with bHLH79 and weakens the bHLH79-MYB10 nuclear module that activates THT genes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot apex, with strongest accumulation in shoot apices of mutants/overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with programmed cell death and shoot apex growth/development control; phenolamides also contribute to defense-related metabolism

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression marker only; no transgenic promoter used
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a marker for onset of cell death during screening of phenolamide-pathway engineering, but not itself a direct engineering target for nutrient metabolism improvement.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HSR203 expression peaks at incipient apex death in Slgb1 mutants, marking cell death associated with high phenolamide accumulation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental death stages at ~5, 10, 15, and 20 DPS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest at 10 DPS in Slgb1 mutant shoot apices during onset of death
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a cell-death marker linking metabolite surge to apex mortality; not a causal metabolic enzyme/regulator in this study.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Marker only; no engineering conclusions established
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cell-death marker gene; family/domain not specified in paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HSR203 transcript abundance strongly increases at the onset of Slgb1 apex death. 2. Its expression peak coincides with maximal N-P-CT and N-FT accumulation. 3. It supports the inference that excess tyramine-derived phenolamides are associated with programmed cell death.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Linked to programmed cell death during apex collapse

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter-driven overexpression in tomato (OE-THT8 lines); recombinant GST fusion expression in E. coli for enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis within phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Do not constitutively overexpress THT8 in tomato because it elevates cytotoxic phenolamides and causes apex death/stunting. For pathway engineering, reduce THT8 expression/edit promoter activity in contexts of excessive phenolamide accumulation; if defense-oriented deployment is considered, use tissue-specific/inducible promoters and monitor growth penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from p-coumaroyl-CoA or feruloyl-CoA to tyramine to form N-p-trans-coumaroyltyramine and N-trans-feruloyltyramine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: THT8 promotes biosynthesis of N-P-CT and N-FT; overexpression raises these phenolamides and induces shoot apex death and stunted growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis, in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with tyramine plus p-coumaroyl-CoA/feruloyl-CoA, transgenic overexpression, LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phenotypes assessed at seedling stage (about 30 DPS for OE lines); developmental stage influences metabolite effects
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript significantly upregulated in Slgb1 mutants; strongly induced by bHLH79/MYB10 overexpression and reduced in myb10 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Tomato shoot apex-associated expression; elevated in mutant and regulatory overexpression contexts
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bHLH79; MYB10 (transcriptional regulators of promoter activity)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq-supported differential expression; targeted LC-MS metabolite quantification; no database accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among 8 tomato THT-like genes, THT8 showed especially strong functional relevance; likely partly redundant with other THT isoforms but sufficient on overexpression to induce deleterious phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tyramine-derived hydroxycinnamoyl amide branch of phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes shoot apex death and stunted growth; likely major growth penalty from excessive phenolamide accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/output step for target phenolamide accumulation, but not definitively rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THT8 catalyzes direct synthesis of N-P-CT and N-FT from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donors and tyramine. 2. THT8 is upregulated in Slgb1 mutants and by bHLH79/MYB10. 3. THT8 overexpression increases target phenolamides and causes shoot apex death, linking enzyme activity to developmental toxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with programmed cell death and defense-related specialized metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter assayed in dual-luciferase; recombinant GST fusion expression in E. coli for enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis within phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for downregulation to reduce harmful N-P-CT/N-FT overaccumulation in shoot apex. Because of probable redundancy, editing may require stacking with THT8 or promoter editing guided by regulatory motifs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from p-coumaroyl-CoA or feruloyl-CoA to tyramine to form N-p-trans-coumaroyltyramine and N-trans-feruloyltyramine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: THT5 contributes to N-P-CT and N-FT biosynthesis and is transcriptionally induced in Slgb1 mutants and by bHLH79/MYB10.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript analysis, recombinant enzyme assay, promoter transactivation by dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assessed mainly at seedling shoot apex stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly upregulated in Slgb1 mutants; activated by bHLH79/MYB10; reduced in myb10 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Tomato shoot apex-associated induction under SlGB1 loss
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq-supported differential expression; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: THT5 and THT8 were the most emphasized THT isoforms in vivo. Promoter contains multiple E-box, AC, and MBS motifs responsive to bHLH79/MYB10.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to toxic phenolamide overaccumulation if overactivated; direct transgenic phenotype not individually tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway contributor; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THT5 is one of the SlGB1-responsive THT genes strongly linked to phenolamide overaccumulation. 2. Recombinant THT5 catalyzes synthesis of N-P-CT and N-FT in vitro. 3. THT5 promoter is activated by bHLH79 and MYB10, especially together.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with cell death and defense-related metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Recombinant GST fusion expression in E. coli for enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis within phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential redundant target to be considered in multiplex editing if stronger suppression of tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis is desired.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes transfer of hydroxycinnamoyl moieties from p-coumaroyl-CoA or feruloyl-CoA to tyramine, generating N-P-CT and N-FT in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Demonstrated acyltransferase activity for N-P-CT and N-FT synthesis in vitro; in vivo developmental role not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not emphasized here; used as positive control in assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized tomato THT; served as positive control in enzyme assays.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown in vivo from this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THT1-3 has robust in vitro activity for N-P-CT and N-FT synthesis. 2. It validates the catalytic capability of tomato THT family members. 3. It supports likely redundancy in tomato tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Recombinant GST fusion expression in E. coli for enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis within phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be included in multiplex suppression strategies because of family redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from p-coumaroyl-CoA or feruloyl-CoA to tyramine to form N-P-CT and N-FT in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows enzymatic activity toward N-P-CT and N-FT formation in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence here is biochemical, not genetic.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THT6 catalyzes synthesis of N-P-CT and N-FT in vitro. 2. It expands the catalytically competent tomato THT repertoire. 3. Functional redundancy likely exists among THT family members.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Recombinant GST fusion expression in E. coli for enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis within phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target in multiplex editing because of pathway redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from p-coumaroyl-CoA or feruloyl-CoA to tyramine to form N-P-CT and N-FT in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows in vitro catalytic activity for target phenolamide formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemical support only in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THT7 is catalytically competent for N-P-CT and N-FT formation in vitro. 2. It supports a multigene THT enzymatic network in tomato. 3. Genetic contribution to apex phenotype was not directly tested.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression/complementation; native locus disrupted by CRISPR/Cas9 or silenced by VIGS
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Condine Red; some assays in Moneymaker and NahG
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SlGB1 is a high-priority architecture and viability regulator. Preserve functional alleles in breeding. If modifying downstream phenolamide pathway, keep SlGB1 intact and tune downstream TF/enzyme expression rather than knocking out SlGB1. Functional orthologs such as Arabidopsis AGB1 can complement in tomato.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of bHLH79-MYB10-driven THT expression and tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis, thereby preventing shoot apex death and maintaining growth/yield.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout, VIGS, complementation, Split-Luc, BiFC, GST pull-down, Co-IP, localization, multiomics, metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream suppressor whose loss causes massive overaccumulation of N-P-CT and N-FT and lethal apex collapse.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage critical; effect strongest around 10 DPS in shoot apices. NahG background showed SA is not sole upstream cause.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed in leaves, roots, stems, and shoot apices; high in shoot apices
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bHLH79; GGB1 mentioned as control without MYB10 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of tyramine-derived phenolamides via regulation of THT transcription
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RNA-seq; proteomics, no public accessions reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction with bHLH79 occurs at plasma membrane/cytoplasm and reduces bHLH79 nuclear accumulation and bHLH79-MYB10 interaction. No direct interaction with MYB10 detected.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes severe developmental defects and yield penalties; excessive suppression of downstream pathway may alter defense-related phenolamides.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: bHLH79-MYB10 complex; indirectly THT5 and THT8
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in heterotrimeric G-protein context; interacts with bHLH79 but does not form canonical MYB-bHLH-WD40 nuclear complex here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: G-protein beta subunit; WD40-repeat protein with 7 WD40 domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated attenuation of TF complex formation and nuclear accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses/attenuates activation of THT5 and THT8 by weakening bHLH79-MYB10 module
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGB1 is the upstream negative regulator preventing excess N-P-CT/N-FT accumulation. 2. It binds bHLH79 outside the nucleus and reduces bHLH79-MYB10 interaction in the nucleus. 3. This suppresses THT5/THT8 expression and protects shoot apex development.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with cell death and developmental regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Not established; SA accumulation tested but not sufficient to explain phenotype

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression; native promoter used in localization and interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for moderating phenolamide accumulation, but overexpression is harmful. Use knockdown/promoter weakening rather than constitutive overexpression if aiming to reduce apex death risk under high-phenolamide backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of THT5/THT8 expression and N-P-CT/N-FT accumulation; overexpression causes shoot apex death and stunted growth.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression lines, LC-MS metabolite profiling, RT-qPCR, Split-Luc, BiFC, GST pull-down with SlGB1, Y2H and Co-IP with MYB10, localization, dual-luciferase promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a key activator within the bHLH79-MYB10 module that directly stimulates THT promoters and increases target phenolamides.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strong effects observed in shoot apices during seedling stage; nuclear abundance reduced by SlGB1 coexpression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed, high in shoot apices; transcript increased >30-fold in Slgb1 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq differential expression; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Present at plasma membrane, cytoplasm, and nucleus. SlGB1 reduces its nuclear fluorescence/protein accumulation, thereby attenuating bHLH79-MYB10 action.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes shoot apex death and growth inhibition due to elevated phenolamide accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms bHLH79-MYB10 transcriptional complex in nucleus; interaction weakened by SlGB1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates THT5 and THT8 promoter activity; stronger activation together with MYB10
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH79 is strongly induced in Slgb1 mutants and positively controls N-P-CT/N-FT accumulation. 2. It interacts with MYB10 in the nucleus and with SlGB1 at membrane/cytoplasm. 3. Overexpression activates THT genes, elevates phenolamides, and causes apex death.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with programmed cell death and growth regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Negatively controlled by SlGB1 at protein interaction/localization level

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression; native locus disrupted by CRISPR/Cas9 for myb10 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MYB10 is a promising downstream target to suppress excessive tyramine-derived phenolamide accumulation and rescue apex defects without altering SlGB1. Loss-of-function or promoter weakening may improve vegetative growth in high-phenolamide contexts, but possible tradeoffs in other phenylpropanoid traits should be monitored.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of THT5/THT8 and N-P-CT/N-FT biosynthesis; overexpression causes shoot apex death and stunting, whereas mutation decreases phenolamides and accelerates apex development and vegetative growth.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout, overexpression, RT-qPCR, LC-MS metabolite profiling, Y2H and Co-IP with bHLH79, dual-luciferase promoter activation, genetic rescue in TRV:SlGB1 background
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical downstream activator required for SlGB1-silencing-induced phenolamide overaccumulation and apex death; myb10 mutation rescues TRV:SlGB1 phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling and vegetative developmental stages; strongest metabolic effects in shoot apex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed in several tissues, high in shoot apices; transcript increased >30-fold in Slgb1 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of tyramine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq differential expression; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct interaction with SlGB1 was detected. Mutation suppresses TRV:SlGB1-triggered apex death and phenolamide increase, placing MYB10 downstream of SlGB1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes shoot apex death and stunted growth; mutation may alter other phenylpropanoid outputs beyond this pathway, though not dissected here.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms transcriptional activation complex with bHLH79 in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates THT5 and THT8 promoter activity and transcript accumulation; acts synergistically with bHLH79
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB10 is a strong positive regulator of N-P-CT/N-FT biosynthesis through THT5/THT8 activation. 2. Overexpression elevates phenolamides and causes apex death, while knockout reduces phenolamides and accelerates growth. 3. MYB10 is genetically downstream of SlGB1 because its mutation rescues SlGB1-silencing defects.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with programmed cell death and developmental control
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Functionally constrained by SlGB1 through inhibition of bHLH79-MYB10 complex formation
